title: Disabling Angular built-in sanitization is security-sensitive
description: Disabling Angular built-in sanitization is security-sensitiveAnalyze your codeconsistency-conventionalsecuritySecurity HotspotcweAngular prevents XSS vulnerabilities by treating all values as untrusted by default. Untrusted values are systematically sanitized by the framework before they are inserted into the DOM.Still, developers have the ability to manually mark a value as trusted if they are sure that the value is already sanitized. Accidentally trusting malicious data will introduce an XSS vulnerability in the application and enable a wide range of serious attacks like accessing/modifying sensitive information or impersonating other users.Ask Yourself WhetherThe value for which sanitization has been disabled is user-controlled.It’s difficult to understand how this value is constructed.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Angular sanitization bypass detected - review for security implications
Disabling Angular's built-in sanitization is security-sensitive and should be avoided
